structure.text:
Subject: NCM-P-2025-11-10 Fannie Mae Removes Minimum Credit Score Requirement for DU Casefiles
From: Nova Capital Markets <capitalmarkets@novahomeloans.com>
Date: 2025-11-10 15:56:14-07:00



ANNOUNCEMENT SUMMARY

Fannie Mae has released new guidance regarding their credit score requirements for loans submitted to DU. Minimum credit score requirements will no longer apply to loans submitted to DU. Desktop Underwriter will no longer apply a minimum credit score but will rely on its own comprehensive analysis of risk factors to determine eligibility. 

 

Additionally, DU nontraditional credit documentation and homebuyer education requirements no longer rely on credit scores. Instead, DU will issue a message when lenders must establish a nontraditional credit history and/or complete homebuyer education when no borrower has at least one credit account or installment account reported on their credit report. 

 

Additional guidance will be communicated as this change evolves. 

 

IMPORTANT NOTES

*	The minimum representative credit score requirement of 620 for loan casefiles for one borrower and minimum average median credit score requirement of 620 for more than one borrower will be removed for new loan casefiles created on or after Nov. 16, 2025. 
*	All other changes apply to loan casefiles submitted or resubmitted on or after the weekend of Nov. 15, 2025
*	NOVA credit score overlays apply and are based off the representative credit score (not the average). 
*	Reach out to the Marketing Team for customized graphics. 

 

ATTACHMENTS

*	Fannie Mae Selling Guide Announcement
*	NOVA Overlays
*	Marketing

** For NOVA Home Loans Internal Distribution Only **

Contact Us:

Scenario Desk <mailto:Scenariodesk@novahomeloans.com>         Lock Desk <mailto:lock.desk@novahomeloans.com>         Product Department <mailto:productdept@novahomeloans.com>
